figures.caption: Figure 2: Adjoint-based design optimization system.
Figure 3: Convergence histories of the residuals related to the mass equation for the cylinder case: a) flow GKS solver; b) adjoint GKS solver.
Figure 4: Flow and adjoint variable contours related to the y-moment equation for the cylinder case.
Figure 5: Verification of the adjoint sensitivities for the cylinder case: a) step size independence study; b) comparison between the adjoint method and FDM.
Figure 6: Aerodynamic shape optimization of the cylinder case: a) evolutionary histories; b) original and optimized shapes.
Figure 7: Static pressure contours in the whole computational domain and Drag on the shape surface: a) pressure; b) drag.
Figure 8: The distributions of p and Cp for the transonic RAE 2822 airfoil: a)in the whole computational domain; b) on airfoil surfaces.
Figure 9: Convergence histories of the residuals related to the mass equation for the RAE 2822 case: a) flow GKS solver; b) adjoint GKS solver.
Figure 10: Both the flow and adjoint variable contours related to the turbulence model equation for the RAE 2822 airfoil.
Figure 11: Drag sensitivity verification for the RAE 2822 case: a) step size independence study; b) sensitivity verification.
Figure 12: Lift sensitivity verification for the RAE 2822 case: a) step size independence study; b) sensitivity verification.
Figure 13: Aerodynamic shape optimization of the RAE 2822: a) evolutionary histories; b) original and optimized airfoils.
Figure 14: Comparison of static pressure and drag on airfoil surfaces for the RAE 2822: a) pressure; b) drag.
Figure 15: Convergence histories of the residuals related to the mass equation for the NACA 0012 case: a) flow GKS solver; b) adjoint GKS solver.
Figure 16: Flow and adjoint variable contours related to the y-moment equation for the NACA 0012 case.
Figure 17: Verification of the adjoint sensitivities for the NACA 0012 case: a) step size independence study; b) comparison between the adjoint method and FDM.
Figure 18: Aerodynamic shape optimization of the NACA 0012: a) evolutionary histories; b) original and optimized airfoils.
Figure 19: Static pressure contours in the whole computational domain: a) original; b) optimized.
Figure 20: Comparison of static pressure and drag on airfoil surfaces for the NACA 0012: a) pressure; b) drag.
Figure 21: Convergence histories of the residuals related to the mass equation: a) flow GKS solver; b) adjoint GKS solver.
Figure 22: Flow and adjoint variable contours related to the y-moment equation for the semicylinder case.
Figure 23: Verification of the adjoint sensitivities for the semi-cylinder case: a) step size independence study; b) comparison between the adjoint method and FDM.
Figure 24: Aerodynamic shape optimization of the semi-cylinder: a) evolutionary histories; b) original and optimized shapes.
Figure 25: Comparison of the static pressure: a) in the whole computational domain; b) at three different locations.
Figure 26: Comparison of the drag (Cd) and pressure (Cp) coefficients on the semi-cylinder surfaces: a) Cd; b) Cp.
